title: ‘We need to revamp our attack’ – Hansi Flick talks transfer plans after Robert Lewandowski confirms Barcelona exit
description: The coach wants a replacement for the legendary striker
fullText:
Robert Lewandowski confirmed on Saturday that he is leaving Barcelona at the end of the season after a legendary four-year run at the club, and Hansi Flick will need to look for a replacement in the summer.

The coach was asked about Barça’s plans to sign a new striker at a press conference on Saturday and recognized it won’t be easy to find a player as good as Lewandowski, but said a “revamp” of the attack is needed as Barça look to next year.

“It won’t be easy to find someone of Robert’s caliber on the market; he’s a player capable of scoring between 25 and 30 goals. I’ve said it many times. We’re going to miss him; that’s life. We need to revamp our attack. He’s been a great role model for the younger players, seeing him work so hard, always there for us in the tough times.

“I don’t think there are any more [farewells], but I don’t think so. Although anything is possible.”

Flick’s last quote indicates that we likely won’t see any other departures of big names despite the rumors about the likes of Jules Kounde and Alejandro Balde, so the focus seems to be to reinforce the attack after Lewy’s exit and maybe add someone else to fortify the defense for next season.

title: Ashford-based guide runs up Mount Rainier and back, shattering speed record
description: The Fastest Known Time for a there-and-back of Washington’s tallest peak is now under four hours.
fullText:
On May 9, Rainier Mountaineering Incorporated (RMI) guide Simon Kearns woke up in company housing in Ashford well before dawn. For breakfast, he had a bowl of oatmeal and a cup of coffee. He’d slept a total of 30 minutes, but that was normal for him before big mountain feats.

He drove up to the Paradise parking lot in Mount Rainier National Park, put on his mountain running gear – including Arcteryx shoes and Black Diamond microspikes – and cued up a French reggae playlist he’d recently been listening to.

At exactly 5 a.m., Kearns looked up at Mount Rainier and then took off for the 14,410-foot summit.

Three hours and fifty-three minutes later, he was back with a record.

There’s a long history of what the outdoor world calls “car to car” records, or FKTs (Fastest Known Times). A “car to car” record is when you start a timer at your car and then run away to climb famous natural features, like El Capitan in Yosemite or entire mountain ranges like the Evolution Traverse on the east side of the Sierra Nevadas, and do not stop your timer until you get back to your car.

The FKT for Mount Rainier was previously held by Alex King, with a time of 4:14 set on June 21, 2021.

Kearns beat King’s record by more than 30 minutes.

Simon Kearns was born in Mancos, Colorado, near Durango, and grew up in the surrounding mountains. His parents are outdoorsy and introduced him to mountain sports like skiing, running and cycling early.

He ran track in high school, then went to college at Colorado University Boulder, where he transferred most of his attention from running to alpinism, skiing and climbing.

Almost immediately after graduating college in 2023, for fun, he grabbed the FKT on Mount Hood (also from King).

“This put me back into the mindset of mountain running again,” Kearns told the News Tribune from his home in Ashford.

Kearns followed the coast up to Washington state, living in his truck and training long link-ups and speed runs in various mountain ranges.

In 2024, he went for the FKT on Mount Rainier for the first time, but got stuck behind a large group guided by the legendary Mount Rainier guiding company, RMI.

“It was frustrating,” Kearns said. “But in chatting with the guide who stopped me, I realized that it actually sounded like a really cool job. So the next year I applied, got hired and moved out to Ashford.”

Kearns says he’s now been on the upper mountain close to 20 times, for guiding and personal recreation.

He attempted another FKT in 2025 but didn’t grab it. He was four minutes too slow.

“I wasn’t stuck behind a group that time, I just didn’t have it, I guess,” Kearns said.

Originally, Kearns was training to break the ski record this spring. But low snowpack and a broken ski inspired him to shift his sights to the foot record instead.

He knew he wanted to go for it in May.

“I was thinking I would go around May 20th, but I was doing a ski project on the mountain – skiing different lines the week before,” Kearns said. “The Thursday before, I went up and skied the mountain, and saw that it was in good shape for running. I took that next day off and decided to just go for it on Saturday.”

Kearns took the Ingraham Direct route, which is the route of choice for RMI in the early season due to being more direct and efficient than Disappointment Cleaver until crevasses and instability render the Ingraham Glacier impassable, usually sometime in June.

The first stretch, the lower part between Paradise and the Muir Snowfield, was soft and slushy. It didn’t freeze enough to be solid, and it hadn’t been warm enough to melt away. But he knew that was the case.

Once he got to the Muir Snowfield, it was nice and smooth, as it usually is this time of year. Later in the year, it gets “chopped up.”

“The lower mountain is faster if you wait until June or July when it’s all melted out, but then the upper mountain tends to be quite a bit slower,” Kearns explained. “So I felt like it was worth it to have a slower mountain down low and faster up high.”

As an employee of RMI, Kearns does a lot of the route work on the mountain – shoveling, ladder placements, etc. As a result, he has a good gauge of where things are and how to approach them the quickest.

Perhaps, most importantly, he has access to their group schedules, so he won’t get caught behind them again.

“I think on the way up, people didn’t know what I was up to,” Kearns told the News Tribune. “When I was coming down, most people were like, ‘Oh, he’s moving pretty quickly, I just saw him go up, and now he’s coming back down already.’ People started cheering and giving words of encouragement.”

Kearns allowed himself about 30 seconds on the summit before heading back down. Once he got back to the parking lot, he crumpled to the ground. A bit more out of disbelief than out of exhaustion. He had the record by over half an hour. And he had the whole rest of the day to enjoy it.

title: Gasperini previews Derby della Capitale
description: Gian Piero Gasperini spoke at a press conference on the eve of the derby. The coach addressed the poor organization surrounding the date and time of the derby and the matches for fourth place: “Clea...
fullText:
Gian Piero Gasperini spoke at a press conference on the eve of the derby.

The coach addressed the poor organization surrounding the date and time of the derby and the matches for fourth place: “Clearly, we’re motivated by this race for fourth place, and we’re the least likely to comment on the chaos surrounding the timetable, because we could have played at any time. It wasn’t a great demonstration of planning, and above all, it’s disappointing for the fans.”

Regarding Dybala’s contract renewal and his condition, the Grugliasco coach said: “The important thing is that both sides, the owners and the player, have this idea. I find it very positive; when there’s a desire on both sides, a solution can be found. He hasn’t been there often this season; that’s just how it went. Now he’s fine and has no problems with his fitness.”

Regarding Lazio, fresh from their Coppa Italia final defeat, Gasperini has no doubts: “Lazio are an excellent team, their only motivation is to thwart Roma, and we have stronger reasons. I expect a real match; we can’t expect them to have extraordinary morale, but they’ll do everything they can to ruin our situation.”

The coach commented on his role in the Giallorossi project: “The club is at the heart of the Roma project; it’s very broad and comprehensive, starting with the stadium and looking to the future. I’m in charge of the technical side, and my responsibilities are limited to building the best possible team and improving it to achieve the best possible goals. The presence of the owners is crucial; everything works better. There’s the possibility of having direct relationships without intermediaries, fewer things are reported, and decisions with the owners are quick. I hope Ryan can build a relationship with these players who are giving their all for this club, regardless of their future.”

Regarding the fact that certain players can be more decisive than others, Gasperini said: “I think that some players, in all teams, if they’re born and raised there, feel it differently once they play in derbies. Perhaps a player arriving from outside isn’t familiar with certain dynamics. Sometimes for these reasons, they don’t perform well, and others excel; for example, Pellegrini often does well in derbies. Lorenzo is coming back from an injury; if he’s fit, I’ll count on him.”

The coach also commented on the very open race for fourth place and the future: “We know that if we win tomorrow, we’re close to reaching our goal. We’ll have to focus only on ourselves and give it our all. Right now, the league is what matters. We’re mathematically guaranteed a spot in the Europa League, and that’s a good achievement. We’ve left Atalanta, Bologna, and Lazio behind. We’re level on points with Milan, close to Juventus and Napoli, and we feel we’ve achieved a good result. However, we must always try to improve. I always thought that third or fourth place would be decided in the end; thinking that second place could be so close was just not an idea.”

Gasperini also spoke about the importance of the club’s presence: “The owners are here to manage the expiring contracts. It could have been a problem for the second half of the season, but the fact that they’re still focused on the season is a great sign of professionalism. I’ve already spoken to El Shaarawy; I’ll tell him that there are still two games left, so he’ll have to stay focused.”

Regarding the importance of the Champions League, the coach said: “Achieving fourth place doesn’t make much difference to me, but I realize that the club is in a huge technical hole. I repeat, I’ve had a great response from the team. It’s been an extraordinary experience, and I hope it ends with a result that exceeds expectations.”

A year ago, fans displayed a banner protesting Gasperini’s possible arrival in the capital; now he’s being cheered. The coach commented on this: “A year ago, they didn’t want me, but I’d like to send that message post-match. I’m beyond grateful; it wasn’t easy to gain credibility from the fans, and from that perspective, it’s been a great season.”

Gasperini expressed little optimism about Dovbyk’s possible return: “Dovbyk is finally back. He’s started again, and it’s a pleasure to see him on the pitch. It’s disappointing to see players sidelined for long periods of time; they work for months and months with their physiotherapists to recover. It’s not easy to imagine him playing tomorrow; he’s been out for a long time. The fact that he’s back playing football is very positive.”

title: 🫢 No shortage of drama in intense Scottish Premiership title decider
description: The 2025/26 Scottish Premiership title decider between Celtic and Hearts lived up to the billing with an enthralling end to the first half. Hearts went to Celtic with a two-point advantage at the top ...
fullText:
The 2025/26 Scottish Premiership title decider between Celtic and Hearts lived up to the billing with an enthralling end to the first half.

Hearts went to Celtic with a two-point advantage at the top of the Premiership and needed just a point to secure the title.

The match started in a tense fashion, with neither side registering a shot on target in the opening 32 minutes.

However, the all-or-nothing bout came to a head late in the first period when Hearts' captain Lawrence Shankland headed in the opener from a few yards out.

Just six minutes later, Celtic were given a golden opportunity to level after Alexandros Kyziridis' handball inside the Hearts penalty area.

Arne Engles stepped up and converted his spot-kick under Alexander Schwolow, sending Celtic Park into delirium.

An intense final 45 minutes of the Scottish Premiership season await Celtic and Hearts. Who will come out on top?

title: 'The evidence was overwhelming' - Carrick deal reached
description: This has been a possible outcome since 25 January, 12 days after Michael Carrick was appointed as Ruben Amorim's replacement ahead of former boss Ole Gunnar Solskjaer.  Carrick had already beaten Manchester City – but Amorim, Solskjaer and others had done that.  When United beat Arsenal at the Emirates thanks to Matheus Cunha's late goal, it was different, the first hint at Carrick bringing something more substantial to Old Trafford.
fullText:
This has been a possible outcome since 25 January, 12 days after Michael Carrick was appointed as Ruben Amorim's replacement ahead of former boss Ole Gunnar Solskjaer.

Carrick had already beaten Manchester City – but Amorim, Solskjaer and others had done that. Despite their reduced status, raising their game against their rivals from across town had not been an issue.

When United beat Arsenal at the Emirates thanks to Matheus Cunha's late goal, it was different, the first hint at Carrick bringing something more substantial to Old Trafford.

For the next three months, the 44-year-old batted away various questions about his future; whether he wanted the job, if he had spoken to the hierarchy about the job, if he was involved in recruitment meetings, whether it was important for potential signings to know who the manager was going to be next season.

At the same time, various candidates for the job were ruled out. Thomas Tuchel signed a contract extension with the Football Association. Carlo Ancelotti moved towards doing the same with Brazil, which is now done. PSG let it be known they were well down the road of agreeing a new deal with Luis Enrique.

Another option, Crystal Palace's Oliver Glasner became embroiled in a dispute with the ownership of the London club that seemed to be heading towards an exit. Palace had already been beaten by non-league Macclesfield in the FA Cup.

All the while, Carrick kept winning matches. Four in a row to start with, six out of seven until a first defeat – at Newcastle – which the club's hierarchy blamed on the players rather than the man in the dugout.

The home defeat by Leeds on 13 April was on the manager, who made a mistake in starting Manuel Ugarte when Kobbie Mainoo was ruled out through injury.

United's response; three successive wins against rivals for a Champions League spot; Chelsea, Brentford and Liverpool, answered key questions about Carrick's suitability for the job.

Ditching Carrick had become a bigger risk than keeping him and shortly United will confirm he has the job.

title: Luciano Darderi apologizes for highly ridiculed Italian Open entrance in Casper Ruud match
description: In case you missed Luciano Darderi had a tremendous run in his home tennis tournament, the Italian Open. That run came to an end on Friday with a semifinal loss to Casper Ruud. Unfortunately, his entrance to the match is…
fullText:
In case you missed Luciano Darderi had a tremendous run in his home tennis tournament, the Italian Open.

That run came to an end on Friday with a semifinal loss to Casper Ruud.

Unfortunately, his entrance to the match is what has overshadowed his performance throughout the tournament including a big upset win over Alexander Zverev.

MORE: Friday’s rainout means lots of great tennis on Saturday at Italian Open

Darderi, 24, was feeling the nerves and adrenalin of the most significant match of his career. He made an entrance, comparable to a prize winning boxer, with sunglasses on and a smile from ear to ear.

Ruud came out before him, stopped and took the hand of the young person who was to escort him on court.

Darderi forgot that part, moving swiftly passed the child awaiting him and never turned back.

To be fair, everyone makes mistakes. It is odd that none of the tournament people mentioned his omission to him, and he could have fixed the error in real time.

MORE: Alexander Zverev accuses Tennis Channel of favoritism

I don’t know much about Darderi, but given the moment, I am willing to give him the benefit of the doubt and some grace.

Darderi issued an Italian social media apology, and hopefully all is forgiven.

He learned a painful lesson, in front of millions of eyes, and he certainly won’t make the same mistake twice.

On top of this, he lost the match 1-6, 1-6 to Ruud so he suffered enough for one day.

title: Arne Slot Says Things Will Get Better for Liverpool at Some Point
description: Liverpool continue to play poorly and get bad results, as they have going back more than a year now.
fullText:
Liverpool started off their Friday evening match against Aston Villa not completely terribly, with Arne Slot’s embattled Reds appearing at least game and competitive on the road against a side level with them on points heading into the penultimate match of the 2025-26 Premier League season.

They even continued to compete after going down a goal. The game, though, started to slip away. Old problems returned. The press was ineffective. Passing was sloppy. Defensive structure was often non-existent. Liverpool didn’t look sharp. There were increasingly frequent gaps for Villa to exploit.

“Damaging because we needed either a win or maybe, maybe, maybe two draws,” Slot said of the result, which adds a little doubt to Liverpool’s hopes of qualifying for the Champions League. “Now we know for sure that we need a win next week if we have to do it ourselves. So that’s really damaging.

“There were parts in the game I felt a result could have gone our way. But I think what people are focused on is the period after 2-1 where we couldn’t find any momentum and we conceded two other goals. It’s frustrating because as a manager you’re responsible if things happen time and time again.”

That, of course, is the sad reality of the situation Liverpool and Slot find themselves in. This keeps happening. It’s now May and this keeps happening. You go back to January of 2025, when Liverpool had largely won the league and began to take their foot off the gas, and ever since this keeps happening.

It’s been 12 months it’s been 15 months it’s been 18 months and it keeps happening. Nothing Slot says after it happens yet again is wrong or bad, perhaps, but at some point people simply stop wanting to hear it. At some point, the fact it keeps happening and you can’t seem to fix it is all that matters.

“I can understand at this moment in time that [the fans] don’t have a lot of confidence or a lot of feeling that things can be much better next season,” Slot added. “But I think they are then underestimating what a window can do, what a new start can do. I think we know quite well what to improve.

“I think one of the things we also have to improve is also very, very obvious. If you miss nine players that can start a game of football and almost all of them are starters for us, then if you add to that in a window, that will automatically lead to much more, but that’s my opinion and no-one has to agree.

“Even today, for large parts of the game we were close to a result but when you are the better team you need to generate more chances and if you have chances you need to score. I don’t think the difference is so big, only doing in a few situations the right thing we would have a massive upward possibility.”

On one hand there might be some truth in that. Having fewer injured players helps. Having in a few new summer signings and better depth helps. Having a break and a fresh start helps. But also it’s been 12 months. It’s been 15 months. It’s been 18 months. And this just keeps happening. Again and again.

title: Newcastle man set for summer exit
description: Newcastle defender Emil Krafth will leave the club when his contract expires this summer.Krafth has made 106 appearances since arriving from French side Amiens in 2019.“I’m so grateful to have had...
fullText:
Newcastle defender Emil Krafth will leave the club when his contract expires this summer.

Krafth has made 106 appearances since arriving from French side Amiens in 2019.

“I’m so grateful to have had the opportunity to represent this incredible football club for the past seven years. It’s been a huge part of my life,” Krafth said.

“I’ve made some amazing memories here and Newcastle is somewhere I’ve been really proud to call home.

“I’ve had up and down moments on a personal level and some difficult times with injury, but I’ve always felt such brilliant support from the fans, my team-mates, the coaching staff and everybody connected to the club. This is something I will remember forever and want to say a massive thank you for.

“It’s been a privilege to wear the shirt and especially to play a part in a team that has managed to make history in the last few years. I believe the future is really bright for this club and I’ll be following the journey closely and continuing to support from a distance.”

title: Clubs express loan interest in Middlesbrough youngster – exclusive
description: Fellow Championship clubs are keen on signing Middlesbrough defender Finley Munroe on loan this summer, Hayters understands.Munroe signed for Boro in January from Aston Villa, following a successful l...
fullText:
Fellow Championship clubs are keen on signing Middlesbrough defender Finley Munroe on loan this summer, Hayters understands.

Munroe signed for Boro in January from Aston Villa, following a successful loan spell at Swindon Town in League Two.

But boss Kim Hellberg has handed the full-back just three Championship appearances since signing.

Munroe is seen as Boro’s left-back for the long-term future, but has found himself behind Matt Targett and Alex Bangura in the pecking order under Hellberg.

Targett is set to return to parent club Newcastle United this summer while Bangura’s long-term future is unclear, meaning Munroe could be line for an increased opportunity next season.

But Boro are considering loan interest in Munroe, who is the cousin of Arsenal and England midfielder Declan Rice, and whether he could benefit from having more guaranteed game time somewhere else.

The 21-year-old came to Middlesbrough with rave reviews from Swindon boss Ian Holloway, who questioned Villa’s decision to let the youngster go on a permanent deal.

“We’ve just lost the quality he gave us down there,” Holloway said after Munroe’s loan spell at the League Two club was cut short. “I can’t get someone from Middlesbrough, who has a four-year contract, who are paying him what they have decided to pay him.

“Well done to Middlesbrough for recognising it. Aston Villa, you should be ashamed of yourselves. You let go one of the best future left-backs I have ever seen in my lifetime, and I have seen a few. Some of them played in the Premier League with me and for me. I think they have made a terrible blunder.”

His future could also be impacted by the outcome of an investigation into alleged spying by Southampton on a Middlesbrough training session.

Boro were beaten by Southampton in the play-off semi-finals, but there remains a possibility that Southampton could be kicked out, which would see Middlesbrough replace them in the final against Hull City at Wembley for a chance at promotion to the Premier League.

title: 🚨 Samuele Inacio, Dortmund's go-to man to seal the Bundesliga
description: Borussia Dortmund have unveiled their latest lineup of the season, and it comes with some major tactical changes. The big surprise in the starting eleven is the presence of young talent Samuele Inacio...
fullText:
Borussia Dortmund have unveiled their latest lineup of the season, and it comes with some major tactical changes.

The big surprise in the starting eleven is the presence of young talent Samuele Inacio, who takes to the pitch as a starter just hours after his contract renewal with the German club was officially confirmed.

Niko Kovac has decided to reward the youngster’s commitment by giving him his chance on a stage of the highest demands.

On the other hand, today’s squad list also stands out for the names waiting for their opportunity on the substitutes’ bench.

Players who are usually key parts of the team’s setup, such as Karim Adeyemi and Julian Brandt , will not be part of the starting lineup, instead being kept in reserve as luxury options for the second half.

The most emotional note of the day comes from Niklas Süle. The experienced centre-back, who has announced his retirement from professional football, will begin this final match of the season on the bench, waiting to play his last minutes as a professional footballer in front of his fans.

This article was translated into English by Artificial Intelligence. You can read the original version in 🇪🇸 here.

title: Vlahovic has been offered to a La Liga team with no Juventus agreement in place
description: Dusan Vlahovic could become a free agent at the end of the season as he has still not signed a new Juventus contract, and he has now been offered to a Spanish club.Juventus are hoping to reach an agre...
fullText:
Dusan Vlahovic could become a free agent at the end of the season as he has still not signed a new Juventus contract, and he has now been offered to a Spanish club.

Juventus are hoping to reach an agreement with him soon after making it clear that they would like to retain him for another season. The Old Lady remains determined to avoid losing one of the most highly regarded strikers in European football, but the situation is becoming increasingly uncertain.

However, delays in securing a new deal could prove costly, as Football Italia reports that Juventus risk losing him to Atletico Madrid.

While Juventus have signalled their intention to extend his contract, progress has reportedly been slow, with discussions yet to reach a final agreement. His representatives are believed to have kept the door open for a possible move elsewhere, depending on how negotiations develop in the coming weeks.

This situation has created concern within Juventus, who are keen to avoid losing a key attacking asset without a transfer fee. The club remain in active discussions, but the lack of a breakthrough has increased speculation regarding his next destination.

Atletico Madrid has now emerged as a potential option, adding further pressure on Juventus to resolve the situation quickly. The Spanish side is said to be monitoring developments closely as they consider strengthening their attacking options ahead of the new season.

Juventus continue to work towards securing Vlahovic’s future at the Allianz Stadium, but the longer the contract situation remains unresolved, the greater the risk of losing him to another European club becomes.

The coming weeks are expected to be decisive, with Juventus needing to accelerate negotiations if they are to prevent the possibility of an exit and retain one of their most important forwards.

title: PGA Championship 2026: Here's the record prize money payout for each golfer at Aronimink
description: The PGA of America annoucned a $1 million increase in the overall prize money payout for the 2026 PGA Championship, raising it to $20 million with the winner earning $3.69 million
fullText:
The rapid rise in prize money payouts in golf have been a provocative topic in all circles of the sport. The launch of LIV Golf and its megamillion-dollar purses forced the PGA Tour to offer more as well, creating exponential leaps that in the short run made sense to stave off a looking competitor but in the long run created questions of fiscal sustainability. Meanwhile, caught in the backdraft were the major championships, previously sporting among the largest paydays in the game only to fall behind and have to make tough decisions on whether they’d increase their purses at a similar rate. New R&A chief Mark Darbon lamented the problem, noting the zero-sum effect that increase in prize money have on funding the governing bodies other initiatives.

Not surprisingly, then, when new PGA of America CEO Terry Clark met with the media for the first time on Wednesday ahead of the PGA Championship, he, too, was asked about purses and how much longer they could continue going up. The topic came with a subtle change in context given LIV Golf’s uncertain future now that the PIF won’t be funding them past this year and whether prize money payout had finally plateaued.

The specific question for Clark was what was his approach in general to making sure the PGA Championship continues competitive with its purse but staying financially responsible? Here’s his response:

“Yeah, it's a balanced approach. … We're really focused on all aspects of how do we improve and continue to look at improvement at the assets we have, including PGA Championships. This is a really important one. Obviously, it's something we look at every year.”

Despite the cautious tone, the PGA of America announced on Saturday morning that this year's prize money payout would be a record $20 million, a $1 million increase from a year ago, with the winner earning $3.69 million. However, this still puts the PGA Championship third highest among the majors behind the U.S. Open ($21.5 million) and the Masters ($21.5 million last month 2026). The Open Championship paid out $17 million at Royal Portrush last July.

Clark, on the job since January, said the decision on prize money isn’t necessarily tied to what the other majors payout or what the PGA Tour does with its $20 million signature events and $25 million Players Championship payday.

“It's not always in comparison to all of those,” Clark said. “It's what are the factor that's make sense. We do look at it as an annual focus around how do we get at competitive purses.”

For the curious, here’s a quick look at the history of the prize money payout at the PGA Championship:

Year: Winner's Pay, Total Purse 1916: $500, $2,580 (first year of the event) 1931: $1,000, $7,200 (first year winner's pay increased) 1953: $5,000, $20,700 (first year winner's pay was $5K) 1958: $5,500, $39,388 (first year of stroke play, also the winner's amount actually decreased that year) 1965: $25,000, $149,700 1978: $50,000, $300,240 1983: $100,000, $608,099 1988: $160,000, $1,000,000 (first year with a $1M total purse) 1993: $300,000, $1,702,750 1998: $540,000, $2,886,800 2000: $900,000, $5,031,100 (first year with a $5M total purse) 2003: $1,080,000, $5,938,300 (first year with $1M-plus to the winner) 2009: $1,350,000, $7,484,500 2014: $1,800,000, $9,913,000 2018: $1,980,000, $11 million 2021: $2,160,000, $12 million 2022: $2,700,000, $15 million 2023: $3,150,000, $17 million 2024: $3,300,000, $18 million 2025: $3,420,000, $19 million

Here's the prize money payout for each golfer this year. Come back after play ends on Sunday and we'll update the list with names and individual paydays:

Players missing the cut and turning in a 36-hole score will be paid $4,300 each. Any player making the cut, but failing to submit a 72-hole score, will also be paid $4,300.

title: West Morgan hires former coach of the year to lead basketball program
description: May 16—TRINITY — The West Morgan Rebels have found their new boys basketball coach. The school made the hire of former Brewer head coach Matthew Crowe official on Tuesday. He replaces previous head coach Bobby Meyer who took the head coaching job at Fairview High School. Crowe spent this past season as an administrator at Fairview but was previously the ASWA Class 5A and Decatur Daily Class ...
fullText:
May 16—TRINITY — The West Morgan Rebels have found their new boys basketball coach.

The school made the hire of former Brewer head coach Matthew Crowe official on Tuesday. He replaces previous head coach Bobby Meyer who took the head coaching job at Fairview High School.

Crowe spent this past season as an administrator at Fairview but was previously the ASWA Class 5A and Decatur Daily Class 5A-7A Coach of the Year after leading Brewer to a 25-7 record and its first Elite Eight appearance in 52 years in 2025.

Now he makes his return to coaching.

"I went out this past season and watched some of my friends coach their teams, and I knew I still had a passion for it," Crowe said. "I had been thinking about (returning to coaching) and was wondering what I was going to do when I saw the opening at West Morgan. I think it's a great opportunity."

Prior to being hired at Brewer in 2022, Crowe was an assistant basketball and football coach at West Morgan. West Morgan Athletic Director Mike Johnson said he'd hoped to one day bring Crowe back and it ended up being the perfect timing to make this hire.

"When he was with us before, he was such a hard-working young man who got a lot out of his kids," Johnson said. "He was the type of coach you hated to lose, so we're really excited to have him back."

When West Morgan's basketball job came open last month, Johnson laid out what he was looking for in a new coach. He said Crowe checks all those boxes.

"You look at all the success we're having in athletics right now, we think a lot of that comes from the emphasis we've put in the weight room," Johnson said. "Then you look at his teams he had at Brewer, they were always strong and physical."

"We also want the kind of leaders that can create a program with kids that do the right thing both on and off the field or court. We feel like he shares a lot of the same values as the other coaches we have," Johnson added.

Crowe will take over a West Morgan team that is coming off a 25-7 season and its third consecutive Elite Eight appearance. The Rebels are returning three starters from that team, including Landon Henderson who averaged 23 points, five rebounds and five assists per game and 96 steals.

It's a far cry from the rebuild Crowe inherited when he took the Brewer job, but he said he's ready to embrace the expectations.

"Expectations are great. I think having high expectations is something that everybody should strive for," Crowe said. "But it's not going to change how we operate. We're still going to try and just improve each day, and keep growing."

Crowe may be a new coach but he says the team he plans to deliver won't be much different from what West Morgan is used to.

"We want to be strong defensively, play fast and play with grit and toughness," he said. "We're going to be passionate and competitive, both in practice and in games."

— caleb.suggs@decaturdaily.com or 256-340-2395. Twitter @CalebSuggs2

title: Report – Inter Put Out-Of-Favor Midfielder Up for Sale
description: Serie A champions Inter have reportedly put out-of-favor midfielder Davide Frattesi up for sale ahead of the summer transfer window. According to Gazzetta dello Sport, the Nerazzurri will listen to of...
fullText:
Serie A champions Inter have reportedly put out-of-favor midfielder Davide Frattesi up for sale ahead of the summer transfer window. According to Gazzetta dello Sport, the Nerazzurri will listen to offers for the former Sassuolo man, with several domestic rivals already vying for his signature.

Despite vowing to reignite Frattesi’s career upon arrival last summer, head coach Cristian Chivu has failed to live up to his promise this season. Instead, Frattesi has played a marginal role in the 45-year-old’s system, making just three Serie A starts.

Furthermore, the 26-year-old has accumulated just 1,133 minutes in all competitions, spanning across 33 appearances. Tired of playing second fiddle to the likes of Hakan Calhanoglu, Nicolo Barella, and Piotr Zielinski, the Italian star has reportedly decided to part ways with Inter at the end of the season.

Meanwhile, Frattesi’s contract at Giuseppe Meazza runs out in the summer of 2028, which puts the Nerazzurri in a decent bargaining position. Therefore, Inter will reportedly entertain offers in the region of €25 million, and there’s no shortage of suitors.

Frattesi scored several pivotal goals as a substitute during Inter’s Scudetto-winning 2023/24 season. However, he has never managed to establish himself as a regular starter at the club, so a summer departure feels like a natural conclusion.

Although his playing time at Meazza has been relatively sparse, Frattesi has drawn a host of inquiries in Serie A and abroad. In addition to long-term admirers Roma, Juventus and Nottingham Forest also displayed their interest in January.

title: Arne Slot tells Liverpool fans they are ‘underestimating’ summer change
description: Liverpool’s 4-2 defeat at Aston Villa has only increased the pressure on Arne Slot.DOWNLOAD THE OFFICIAL EMPIRE OF THE KOP APP FOR ALL THE LATEST & BREAKING UPDATES – STRAIGHT TO YOUR PHONE! ON A...
fullText:
Liverpool’s 4-2 defeat at Aston Villa has only increased the pressure on Arne Slot.

DOWNLOAD THE OFFICIAL EMPIRE OF THE KOP APP FOR ALL THE LATEST & BREAKING UPDATES – STRAIGHT TO YOUR PHONE! ON APPLE & GOOGLE PLAY

We had all but secured Champions League football weeks ago, but repeated stumbles mean the final day now comes with nerves rather than simply being a farewell to Mo Salah and Andy Robertson.

Despite that, the Dutchman remains confident that this situation can look very different next season.

Speaking in his post-match press conference, via Sky Sports News on X, Slot accepted why supporters are finding it difficult to feel positive right now.

The Liverpool boss said: “I can understand at this moment of time that they don’t have a lot of confidence or a lot of feeling that things can be much better next season, but I think that they are underestimating what a window can do, what a new start can do.”

He added: “I think we know quite well what to improve. I think one of the things we have to improve is also very, very, very obvious.”

That will be read by many as a reference to the defensive problems that have plagued Liverpool all season.

We have now conceded 52 Premier League goals, a dreadful total for any side defending the title and chasing Champions League football.

Slot also pointed to the injury situation, saying: “If you miss nine players that can start a game of football, and almost all of them are starters for us or have been for a large part of the season starters for us, then if you add that to what you can improve in a window… that will automatically lead to much more.”

There is truth in that, but Jermaine Pennant’s furious reaction after Villa shows how little patience there is, with the former winger saying Slot “has to go” after another damaging result.

Robertson’s own message was more measured but just as telling, as the Scot admitted the performance was “a long way short of the levels of this club.”

Slot may be trusted with the summer, but the question now is whether supporters still trust him to use it properly.

title: Morecambe release 18 players following relegation
description: Morecambe release 18 players following their relegation from the National League.
fullText:
Morecambe have released 18 players following their relegation from the National League.

The Shrimps were relegated to the National League North having finished 22nd in 2025-26, their second relegation in as many seasons.

Terrell Agyemang, Jamal Blackman, Ma'kel Campbell, Raheem Conte, Harlee Dean, Joshua Eppiah, Ludwig Francillette, Kyle Jameson, Archie Mair, Admiral Muskwe, Joe Nuttall, Daniel Ogwuru, Harry Panayiotou, Mohammed Sangare, Alie Sesay, Yann Songo'o, George Thomas and Ben Tollitt are all set to leave at the end of their contracts.

Miguel Azeez, Lennon Dobson and Liam Hogan are under contract for next season, while the club have opted to extend Adam Fairclough and Alfie Scales' contracts.

"Everyone at Morecambe Football Club would like to place on record our sincere thanks to all departing players for their efforts, professionalism and commitment during their time at the Mazuma Mobile Stadium, and we wish them every success in the future," the club said.

title: Mets lose rotation anchor as Clay Holmes suffers fractured fibula on line drive
description: Starting pitcher Clay Holmes, one of the few bright spots for the last-place Mets this season, suffered a devastating setback Friday after fracturing his right fibula when he was struck by a 111.1 mph comebacker off Spencer Jones’ bat.  Holmes immediately went down after taking the line drive on his lower right leg, and the severity of the injury became clear as medical staff rushed to evaluate him on the field.  The Mets later confirmed the fracture, announcing that Holmes will be sidelined indefinitely.
fullText:
Mets lose rotation anchor as Clay Holmes suffers fractured fibula on line drive originally appeared on The Sporting News. Add The Sporting News as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

Starting pitcher Clay Holmes, one of the few bright spots for the last-place Mets this season, suffered a devastating setback Friday after fracturing his right fibula when he was struck by a 111.1 mph comebacker off Spencer Jones’ bat. The injury occurred during the top of the fourth inning and leaves New York without one of its most consistent and productive performers.

Holmes immediately went down after taking the line drive on his lower right leg, and the severity of the injury became clear as medical staff rushed to evaluate him on the field. The Mets later confirmed the fracture, announcing that Holmes will be sidelined indefinitely. The loss represents another difficult chapter in what has already been a disappointing and frustrating season for the club.

Despite the team’s struggles, Holmes had emerged as one of the brightest stories in the organization. In his second season as a starting pitcher after transitioning from a bullpen role with the Yankees, he had become a legitimate All-Star candidate. Through his first eight starts of the season, Holmes posted an outstanding 1.86 ERA, establishing himself as one of the National League’s most effective arms.

Holmes’ transformation into a starter has been one of baseball’s more impressive career adjustments. After spending years as a dependable reliever and serving in the Yankees bullpen, he signed a three-year, $38 million contract with the Mets before the 2025 season and successfully made the leap to the rotation.

Entering Friday, Holmes ranked third in the National League with his 1.86 ERA. His absence now creates a significant void in the Mets’ rotation and raises questions about how the struggling team will replace one of its few reliable contributors.

For a team searching for positives amid a difficult campaign, losing Holmes could prove especially painful. Beyond his production, he provided stability and optimism every fifth day, giving the Mets a dependable presence and a reason for fans to remain encouraged during a challenging season.

title: Former interim boss Foley among six leaving Barrow
description: Captain Niall Canavan and Sam Foley, who ended the season in interim charge, will leave relegated Barrow this summer.
fullText:
Club captain Niall Canavan and midfielder Sam Foley, who ended the season in interim charge of the first team, are in a group of six players who will leave relegated Barrow this summer.

Foley was the fifth man to oversee a first-team game during the 2025-26 campaign, having been given temporary control for the remainder of the season following Dino Maamria's exit in March.

Barrow won two of their 11 matches under the 39-year-old and finished bottom of League Two to be relegated to the National League.

Adam Murray left Kidderminster Harriers earlier this week to become Barrow's new boss on a two-year contract.

Defender Canavan, 35, joined the Bluebirds in January 2022 and made 183 appearances for the club.

Connor Mahoney, Tyler Walker, Kane Hemmings and Angus MacDonald will also depart at the end of their respective contracts this summer.

However, the club has triggered an option to extend goalkeeper Wyll Stanway's contract until next summer.

title: Napoli to lean on Kevin De Bruyne for their clash with Pisa
description: Napoli are boosted by the return of Kevin De Bruyne as they look to bounce back from defeat to Bologna last time out in their penultimate match of the Serie A season at relegated Pisa on Sunday. Gli Azzurri’s place as runners-up looked less secure after they slipped to a 3-2 loss at home to Bologna on Monday – a win could have sent them six points clear of fifth-placed Roma in their bid to secure Champions League football.
fullText:
Napoli are boosted by the return of Kevin De Bruyne as they look to bounce back from defeat to Bologna last time out in their penultimate match of the Serie A season at relegated Pisa on Sunday.

Gli Azzurri’s place as runners-up looked less secure after they slipped to a 3-2 loss at home to Bologna on Monday – a win could have sent them six points clear of fifth-placed Roma in their bid to secure Champions League football.

Antonio Conte’s side currently sit five points ahead of Como in sixth, although it would still take an extraordinary sequence of results for them to miss out at this stage.

Belgium midfielder De Bruyne is expected to be back in the starting line-up after suffering a cut to his eyebrow in a collision during a training session last Sunday that required four stitches, according to the Italian media.

Napoli winger Matteo Politano misses out through suspension, while Brazil international David Neres – who has been sidelined since January following ankle surgery – faces a late fitness test.

Kosovo captain and defender Amir Rrahmani made his 200th appearance for the club at the start of the week and should keep his place.

With just two wins all season, bottom side Pisa will hope to end a seven-match losing streak.

The hosts, who were relegated to Serie B after a 2-1 defeat to Lecce earlier this month, will be without suspended duo Felipe Loyola and Rosen Bozhinov for the match.

title: Lewandowski to leave Barcelona at the end of the season
description: Robert Lewandowski's time at Barcelona is almost over, with the Spanish champions not offering the former Bayern Munich striker a new contract for next season. Barcelona posted on social media on Saturday: "Came as a star, leaves as legend.
fullText:
Robert Lewandowski's time at Barcelona is almost over, with the Spanish champions not offering the former Bayern Munich striker a new contract for next season.

Barcelona posted on social media on Saturday: "Came as a star, leaves as legend."

The Poland international played four years for Barcelona and won the Spanish league title three times. It is still unclear what the 37-year-old will do next.

"After four years full of challenges and hard work, it's time to move on. I leave with the feeling that the mission is complete. 4 seasons, 3 championships," the FIFA men's best player 2020 and 2021 wrote on social media.

"I will never forget the love I received from the fans from my very first days. Catalonia is my place on earth."

Lewandowski has scored 13 goals so far this season. Two matchdays remain in Spain's La Liga. Barcelona play Real Betis on Sunday.

title: Report – Juventus Make 3-Man Shortlist if ‘Dream’ Target Bernardo Silva Fails Join
description: Serie A heavyweights Juventus are reportedly hellbent on signing outgoing Manchester City stalwart Bernardo Silva in the upcoming transfer window. However, according to Calciomercato.it, the Turin gia...
fullText:
Serie A heavyweights Juventus are reportedly hellbent on signing outgoing Manchester City stalwart Bernardo Silva in the upcoming transfer window. However, according to Calciomercato.it, the Turin giants have compiled a three-man shortlist if they fail to secure the 31-year-old’s signature.

Bernardo Silva will be out of contract in June, with the former Monaco star already announcing his summer departure. Indeed, the Portuguese midfielder will sever ties with Manchester City after nine years. Meanwhile, he has emerged as one of Juventus’ top targets heading into the summer.

Juventus boss Luciano Spalletti is desperate to bolster his midfield options. Despite earmarking Inter outcast Davide Frattesi as one of his highest priorities, the former Scudetto winner is also keen to lure Silva to the Allianz Stadium. However, the record-time Italian champions are facing stiff competition from several wealthy foreign clubs in the race to sign the Man City ace.

Therefore, Juventus are already preparing for a worst-case scenario.

Brahim Diaz has caught Spalletti’s attention, with the diminutive forward ready to pack his bags and leave Real Madrid after failing to secure regular first-team football at the Santiago Bernabeu. Diaz is no stranger to Serie A, having featured for Milan between 2020 and 2023.

In addition to the 26-year-old Moroccan, Juventus are keeping close tabs on Lorenzo Pellegrini’s situation. Indeed, the Italian midfielder’s contract runs out in just over a month, with Roma unable to convince him to put pen to paper on a new deal. However, Inter are also in the mix.

Last but not least, Juventus have placed a watchful eye on Udinese’s rising star, Arthur Atta. Fresh off a superb Serie A campaign with the Friulians, the Frenchman is ready to take his career to the next step. Yet, he won’t come cheap, with Kosta Runjaic’s side slapping a €25 million price tag on their prized asset.

title: Roma midfielder Cristante hails ‘devastating’ teammate Malen after permanent switch from Aston Villa
description: Bryan Cristante has heaped praise on Donyell Malen ahead of Sunday’s Derby della Capitale, describing the Dutch forward as a devastating presence inside the penalty area just days after Roma closed ...
fullText:
Bryan Cristante has heaped praise on Donyell Malen ahead of Sunday’s Derby della Capitale, describing the Dutch forward as a devastating presence inside the penalty area just days after Roma closed in on making his move from Aston Villa permanent due to their, at minimum, qualification for the Europa League.

Speaking to Serie A’s official channels, via TuttoMercatoWeb, the Roma midfielder was effusive about the impact Malen has made since joining on loan in January.

“He is strong, inside the area he is devastating,” Cristante said. “He has quickness in his control, in moving the ball, in shooting, and he is composed in front of goal. For us he is an extra weapon. We are keeping him close and we hope he continues like this in these last two matches.”

PARMA, ITALY – MAY 10: Donyell Malen of AS Roma celebrates scoring his team’s first goal during the Serie A match between Parma Calcio 1913 and AS Roma at Stadio Ennio Tardini on May 10, 2026 in Parma, Italy. (Photo by Alessandro Sabattini/Getty Images)

Cristante also reflected on Roma’s collective growth under Gian Piero Gasperini. “We were good at following the coach even in difficult moments,” he said. “We always stayed on his wavelength and did what he asked. With time we have learned his patterns more and more. Our strength has been never abandoning the coach’s ideas.”

On the Champions League race, Cristante was determined. “It is our objective and we have had a great season, it would be a shame to throw it away at the end,” he said. “It does not depend only on us, but we will give everything to get back into the Champions League. We deserve it.”

The Roma vice-captain was equally thoughtful on the derby itself, cautioning against letting emotion override clarity. “The derby is always the derby, form does not matter,” he said.

“Both teams will give something extra. You need a good mix of everything to win it. If you put in too much heart you lose the clarity to win, and vice versa. You need to stay balanced while giving that something extra.”

title: The hidden threat to Oklahoma City’s hot start: Jalen Williams’ hamstring explains it all
description: Oklahoma City hasn’t put a foot wrong this postseason. The Thunder swept the Lakers to extend their perfect start to 8-0, but that run has only added more focus on the next big question facing the team.
fullText:
Oklahoma City hasn’t put a foot wrong this postseason. The Thunder swept the Lakers to extend their perfect start to 8-0, but that run has only added more focus on the next big question facing the team.

This group is built on rhythm, and right now, Jalen Williams’ return timeline is the only thing that could disrupt it.

Williams has been out with a Grade 1 left hamstring strain, missing six playoff games so far. But with how well Oklahoma City handled business in his absence, they have time to let him heal properly without rushing anything.

Williams isn’t just an easy addition. He’s one of their top scorers and a key two-way player, but he’s also missed extended time this season. The Thunder have had to adapt without him, so this isn’t about waiting for someone to come in and steady things – it’s about deciding the right moment to reintroduce him into a team that’s already found its groove.

Latest reports still list him as week-to-week, with his return depending on how well he handles increased on-court activity. That approach makes sense for a hamstring issue – you can estimate when the pain might subside, but only real basketball movements will show if he’s ready for the playoff demands of stopping and starting at full speed.

What makes this injury watch more interesting is how Ajay Mitchell has stepped into the gap. He’s given the Thunder another downhill guard, a secondary creator, and someone who fits right into their low-mistake offensive style. That doesn’t make him a better player than Williams, but it does shift the conversation from when Williams should return to how he should be worked back in.

Mark Daigneault doesn’t need to force anything here. The Thunder have plenty of defence, ball pressure, and playmaking around Shai Gilgeous-Alexander to keep things steady. That allows for a slower ramp-up, limited minutes, and fewer forced touches if Williams is cleared during the conference finals.

Where most teams might feel pressure to rush a star back into the lineup, the Thunder can afford to be patient. They’ve built enough of a cushion that there’s no need to force anything, and with hamstring injuries, that’s usually the best approach.

That leaves Williams’ return as the only real question mark in what’s been an otherwise flawless run so far. Oklahoma City doesn’t need him to come back as a saviour. They just need him back at the right time, so his return doesn’t disrupt what’s already working.

title: Lewandowski to leave Barcelona as 'mission is complete'
description: Poland striker Robert Lewandowski will leave Barcelona at the end of the season when his contract expires.
fullText:
Robert Lewandowski says he is leaving Barcelona this summer at the end of his contract.

The 37-year-old joined from Bayern Munich in 2022 and has scored 119 goals in 191 games, helping Barca win three La Liga titles, including this season, and the 2025 Copa del Rey.

Lewandowski said on Instagram: "After four years full of challenges and hard work, it's time to move on.

"I leave with the feeling that the mission is complete. Four seasons, three championships."

The Poland international has had a number of injuries over the past year and started only 15 league games this season.

He made a late appearance in last weekend's Clasico win over Real Madrid which saw Barca confirmed as champions, but his goal in the 2-1 win at Osasuna earlier in the month helped set them up for the title success.

Last December, Chicago Fire said they had talks with Lewandowski over a move to Major League Soccer.

"Barca is back where it belongs," he added. "I will never forget the love I received from the fans from my very first days. Catalonia is my place on earth."

His final game for the club will be at the Spotify Camp Nou against Real Betis on Sunday, with Barca ending their campaign the following Saturday at Valencia.

Coach Hansi Flick, who also coached Lewandowski at Bayern Munich, has confirmed Lewandowski will start against Betis.

"He spoke with the team, said goodbye, and that it was a great time for him here in Barcelona," said Flick.

"I've worked with Robert now for years, and we won together nine [trophies] - all the titles I've won were with him.

"For me it's a privilege to work with him. He's a professional player. Every day he gave the best with his body to be at the highest level.

"He's a perfect role model for young players - a fantastic human, but also a world-class player. This is why he's still playing at this level."

Barcelona also paid tribute to the forward in a statement as they added: "Thank you, Robert Lewandowski, for every goal, every [bit of] fight, and every magical moment wearing these colours. He arrived as a star, he leaves as a legend."

Listen to the latest Football Daily podcast

title: McGregor Vs. Holloway 2 Reportedly Stalled By Main Event Stipulation
description: An official announcement for Conor McGregor’s long-awaited return fight appears imminent, but it may not come in time to spoil tonight’s Ronda Rousey vs. Gina Carano superfight on Netflix. According to a report by the New York Post, a deal between Conor McGregor and Max Holloway is expected to be finalized for UFC 329 on […]
fullText:
An official announcement for Conor McGregor’s long-awaited return fight appears imminent, but it may not come in time to spoil tonight’s Ronda Rousey vs. Gina Carano superfight on Netflix.

According to a report by the New York Post, a deal between Conor McGregor and Max Holloway is expected to be finalized for UFC 329 on July 11 in Las Vegas, Nevada, over the next week. Some originally speculated that UFC CEO Dana White has been planning to announce the return of “Notorious” this weekend in effort to take some shine away from a feisty Rousey. That would be pretty savage on behalf of UFC, but pettier things have happened in the world of mixed martial arts (MMA).

Earlier this week, long-time MMA reporter, Ariel Helwani, revealed that a McGregor vs. Holloway rematch was “on the goal line,” but that didn’t give fight fans a ton of information to work with. Friday’s report by New York Post provided some extra details for McGregor’s potential UFC comeback, which has been nearly five years in the making following his last fight in July 2021.

Per New York Post, McGregor vs. Holloway is expected to take place at 170 pounds. Many expected that to be the case given how big McGregor gets when he’s not fighting, but this will be Holloway’s first appearance at welterweight. The former UFC featherweight champion just moved up to lightweight last year and has produced a 1-1 record ever since, most recently losing his “BMF” title to Charles Oliveira at UFC 326.

In addition, it appears McGregor’s Team is still pushing hard for his return fight to be three rounds instead of five. Keep in mind, McGregor vs. Holloway 2 would certainly be asked to headline UFC’s International Fight Week this July. As we all know, UFC main events are five rounds with rarely any exceptions. McGregor would be one of the only fighters to get such an exception, but it begs the question if “Notorious” is in the best shape he can be for his return.

Only time will tell when McGregor’s return will be made official, but it sounds like it could happen at any point over the next week.

title: 🚨 Kompany rests Olise for Goretzka's farewell
description: Bayern head into the final Bundesliga matchday with their eyes on the Pokal final. For this reason, coach Vincent Kompany has decided to rest Michael Olise, one of the standout players of the season, ...
fullText:
Bayern head into the final Bundesliga matchday with their eyes on the Pokal final.

For this reason, coach Vincent Kompany has decided to rest Michael Olise, one of the standout players of the season, to avoid any physical setback ahead of the cup clash.

The big surprise in Bayern’s starting XI is Lennart Karl. The very young academy product will take Olise’s place. The match against Köln will also carry a strong emotional weight.

Leon Goretzka will start in what has been confirmed as his final official match in a Bayern Munich shirt, as he will leave the club this coming summer.

The Allianz Arena is therefore getting ready to say goodbye to one of its key figures of recent years and, at the same time, welcome one of the brightest prospects from its academy in a match full of intrigue.

title: 😮 Never happened before: will the Bundesliga season end in a play-off?
description: Today is the very last matchday of this year’s Bundesliga season. In just a few hours, we will finally know which teams are relegated, who secures the Champions League tickets, and in what position ...
fullText:
Today is the very last matchday of this year’s Bundesliga season. In just a few hours, we will finally know which teams are relegated, who secures the Champions League tickets, and in what position the clubs will finish the campaign.

There is, however, one scenario in which the ranking of two clubs would still have to be decided even after today’s final whistle. Of course, that is pretty unlikely, but during Heidenheim’s comeback run at the latest, we were once again reminded that miracles are definitely possible on the big football stage and that there are stories only sport can write.

This season could end with a playoff match at a neutral venue for the very first time ever.

If Gladbach beat TSG Hoffenheim 5–4 and HSV draw 4–4 with Leverkusen, both clubs would have the same number of points, the same goal difference, the same number of goals scored, an even head-to-head record, the same number of away goals scored in the head-to-head, and the same total number of away goals scored overall.

So who would get the higher placing then, which is important because TV money, among other things, depends heavily on it?

According to a report by the ‘Rheinische Post’, it would not come down to the fair play table or anything similar; according to the regulations, it would actually lead to a playoff match at a neutral venue.

That would be a truly extraordinary scenario.

What do you think? Would you like to see such a playoff match, even if, from a sporting perspective, there is not all that much at stake anymore? Feel free to write it in the comments!

title: Chelsea vs. Man City live score: FA Cup final result, updates, stats from Wembley
description: Manchester City are in their fourth consecutive FA Cup final but have lost the past two. Can Pep Guardiola's Carabao Cup winners complete a domestic double against Chelsea?
fullText:
Chelsea vs. Man City live score: FA Cup final result, updates, stats from Wembley originally appeared on The Sporting News. Add The Sporting News as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

Chelseavs. Man City FA Cup final live score

Latest updates, highlights and commentary

Manchester City are seeking to complete England's domestic cup double when they take on Chelsea in the FA Cup final.

Pep Guardiola's side beat Arsenal 2-0 in March's Carabao Cup final thanks to a Nico O'Reilly brace.

They are unbeaten against English opposition since the Manchester derby back in January, and victory would see Guardiola lift the 20th trophy of his City tenure. It would also keep his team on track to repeat their 2018/19 domestic treble, although the destiny of the title lies firmly in Arsenal's hands.

Chelsea's form this season has collapsed, with FA Cup matches standing as their only wins in the past 12 matches across all competitions.

Interim head coach Calum McFarlane did manage to mastermind a 1-1 draw at the Etihad Stadium during his first temporary spell in charge, however, and Chelsea held Liverpool to the same scoreline at Anfield last week.

The London club are eight-time FA Cup winners, one ahead of City in the all-time rankings. Guardiola's team are the first to reach four consecutive finals, although they have lost their past two.

The Sporting News is following the FA Cup final live, providing score updates and commentary

Venue: Wembley Stadium (London, UK)
Referee: Darren England

Chelsea (3-4-2-1, right to left): Sanchez (GK) – Colwill, Fofana, Hato – James, Caicedo, Santos, Cucurella – Palmer, Fernandez – Pedro

Man City (4-2-3-1 right to left): Trafford (GK) — Nunes, Khusanov, Stones, O'Reilly — Foden, Silva — Semenyo, Cherki, Doku — Haaland

1 hr 30 mins before kickoff: Plenty of observers have remarked that this isn't exactly an FA Cup final teeming with the old magic. But what about the prospect of Calum McFarlane, the rookie Chelsea interim manager and former Manchester City youth coach picking up the trophy at the expense of his old employers and arguably the best manager in modern football history in Pep Guardiola? Doesn't that whet your whistle?

If Chelsea are to get the job done they'll have to overturn wretched runs of form, both in the immediate past and in this fixture over the past five years. The last time the Blues won a non-FA Cup game was 12 matches ago against Aston Villa on March 4. Liam Rosenior, remember him? Anyway, Chelsea are also winless in 13 matches against City, losing 10. Their last win? Only the 2021 Champions League final...

2 hours before kickoff. Hello and welcome to The Sporting News' live coverage of Chelsea vs. Manchester City in the 145th FA Cup final.

The FA Cup final kicks off in London at 3 p.m. local time.

Here's how that time translates across some of the major territories:

The two clubs have met on 182 previous occasions with Chelsea just holding the historic win advantage — 71 to 69.

However, the Blues haven't beaten City since the last time they met in a final when Thomas Tuchel won the 2021 Champions League.

In the 13 games since, City have won 10, including twice in the FA Cup.

After deploying a back three for the draw at Liverpool, Calum McFarlane is likely to stick with a similar formation here.

Reece James is very likely to replace Malo Gusto and captain the Blues at Wembley.

Levi Colwill could start in a remarkable turn of events after missing almost the entire season with an ACL injury.

Pep Guardiola is certain to bring back all of his big guns after resting the likes of Erling Haaland and Jeremy Doku for the win over Palace.

Rodri has not played since the win over Arsenal last month and remains a doubt.

Marc Guehi is free to play because the FA changed its rules at the start of the season, allowing players to play for two clubs in one campaign. He had already featured in Palace's shock defeat to Macclesfield having captained the Eagles to the trophy 12 months ago.

However, long-serving centre-back John Stones could be granted a farewell start at centre-back.

Here is how to watch the match in some of the world's major regions:

title: Barcelona confirm Lewandowski exit amid Milan & Juventus links
description: Robert Lewandowski has officially confirmed his departure from Barcelona at the end of the season, leaving the club as a free agent and opening the door for Milan and Juventus to make their moves for ...
fullText:
Robert Lewandowski has officially confirmed his departure from Barcelona at the end of the season, leaving the club as a free agent and opening the door for Milan and Juventus to make their moves for the Polish striker.

The 37-year-old announced the news via a video on his personal social media channels, with Barcelona responding warmly on their own platforms.

“He arrived as a star. He leaves as a legend,” the club wrote, celebrating a four-year spell that produced 119 goals and 24 assists in 191 appearances. Lewandowski won three La Liga titles, one Copa del Rey and a Spanish Super Cup during his time in Catalonia.

BARCELONA, SPAIN – FEBRUARY 28: Robert Lewandowski of FC Barcelona celebrates scoring his team’s fourth goal during the LaLiga EA Sports match between FC Barcelona and Villarreal CF at Spotify Camp Nou on February 28, 2026 in Barcelona, Spain. (Photo by Alex Caparros/Getty Images)

In his own message, Lewandowski was emotional.

“After four years full of challenges and hard work, the time has come to turn the page,” he wrote. “I leave with the feeling of having completed my mission. I will never forget the affection I received from the fans from my very first days. Catalonia is my place in the world.”

Sunday’s home match against Betis will be his final Camp Nou appearance.

With his contract expiring in June, both Milan and Juventus had already been monitoring the situation closely, and now that his departure is confirmed, the race to sign one of world football’s most decorated strikers as a free agent begins in earnest.

title: Why Marcelo understands Enzo’s Spain decision despite Brazil ties
description: Marcelo has spoken about his son Enzo Alves choosing to represent Spain at youth level rather than Brazil, and the reaction around the decision is easy to understand. Enzo is Marcelo’s son, so Brazil will always be the first country many people connect with him.
fullText:
Marcelo has spoken about his son Enzo Alves choosing to represent Spain at youth level rather than Brazil, and the reaction around the decision is easy to understand.

Enzo is Marcelo’s son, so Brazil will always be the first country many people connect with him. That is natural when his father is one of the most recognisable Brazilian footballers of his generation.

But this is also where the story needs balance. Enzo’s decision feels surprising emotionally, yet it is not illogical when his own life and football background are considered.

Marcelo’s connection with Brazil is obvious. He was not just a Brazilian player who had a good career in Europe, he became a global name while carrying that identity throughout his time at the top level.

That is why Enzo choosing to represent Spain creates such an instant talking point. The surname points people towards Brazil before they have even looked at the player’s own story.

Marcelo appears to understand that emotional pull. He has spoken about Enzo’s decision by explaining that his son was born in Spain, that his life is there, and that what matters most is that he is happy.

That is the important distinction. This does not need to be treated as a dramatic rejection of Brazil, because Enzo’s own football life has not followed Marcelo’s path from the start.

The clearest reason Spain makes sense is simple. Enzo was born in Madrid, grew up in Spain and joined Real Madrid’s academy in 2017.

That matters more than the outside expectation created by his father’s nationality. International football can be emotional, but it is also personal, and Enzo’s personal football education has been Spanish.

He is also building his own identity as a player. Enzo is a 16-year-old striker, which already separates him from Marcelo’s image as one of the great attacking left-backs.

There is no need to force him into a legacy that does not quite match his own development. The family name makes the story bigger, but the player has a separate route.

Real Madrid clearly see something worth developing too. Enzo signed his first professional contract with the club in January 2026, which underlines how serious his current pathway already is.

The most interesting part of this story is not simply that Enzo has picked Spain. It is that Marcelo can feel the Brazilian side of the decision while still recognising the logic behind it.

That is a normal reaction from a father and a former Brazil international. He does not have to pretend the choice carries no emotion, but he also does not need to turn it into a public problem.

Enzo’s Spain decision is best viewed through that same lens. It is surprising because of Marcelo, but understandable because of Enzo.

There is no need to make the story heavier than that. For now, Enzo is following the country where he was born, raised and developed as a footballer.

Marcelo’s legacy makes that feel strange. Enzo’s life makes it make sense.

Why FIFA endured a $240 million loss on China broadcast deal ahead of 2026 World Cup

title: Confirmed lineups: FC St. Pauli vs. VfL Wolfsburg
description: FC St. Pauli (6-8-19, 18th) host VfL Wolfsburg (6-8-19, 16th) at Millerntor-Stadion in Hamburg, in the thirty-fourth and final matchweek of the 2025/26 Bundesliga season.The two sides are both involve...
fullText:
FC St. Pauli (6-8-19, 18th) host VfL Wolfsburg (6-8-19, 16th) at Millerntor-Stadion in Hamburg, in the thirty-fourth and final matchweek of the 2025/26 Bundesliga season.

The two sides are both involved in a battle for sixteenth place and the lone relegation playoff spot. 1. FC Heidenheim 1846 is also involved, as all three teams are tied on 26 points going into the final day. VfL Wolfsburg have a -26 goal difference (42-68), while 1. FC Heidenheim 1846 (41-70) and FC St. Pauli (28-57) are both on -29 goal differences.

If FC St. Pauli win, they need to hope that Heidenheim draw or lose their match with 1. FSV Mainz 05, which kicks off at the exact same time. If Heidenheim defeat Mainz, then St. Pauli must hope that the Heidenheim win is not by an equal or greater margin than what they won by.

A draw means that VfL Wolfsburg qualify for the relegation playoff, unless Heidenheim defeat Mainz - in which Heidenheim would overtake Wolfsburg and both sides at Millerntor would get relegated.

A VfL Wolfsburg win means almost certain qualification for the relegation playoff, since they have a three-goal advantage when it comes to goal differential going in. However, if Heidenheim win by a greater margin of three goals or more than what Wolfsburg won by (for example, Wolfsburg win 1-0 and Heidenheim win 4-0), then Heidenheim qualifies on goals scored and Wolfsburg would be relegated.

Here are the confirmed lineups for this key relegation matchup.

FC St. Pauli (3-4-1-2): Vasilj - Dźwigała, Wahl, Andō - Oppie, Chima Fujita, Irvine, Pyrka - Metcalfe - Hountondji, Kaars.

VfL Wolfsburg (3-3-2-2): Grabara - Koulierakis, Vavro, Belocian - Mæhle, Souza, Kumbedi - Eriksen, Svanberg - Pejčinović, Daghim.

title: Pep Guardiola provides an answer on his future of today’s FA Cup final
description: Yesterday saw Pep Guardiola say that today’s FA Cup final between Manchester City and Chelsea will not be his final trip as City manager to Wembley. However, Pep Guardiola’s answer to the question...
fullText:
Yesterday saw Pep Guardiola say that today’s FA Cup final between Manchester City and Chelsea will not be his final trip as City manager to Wembley. However, Pep Guardiola’s answer to the question of whether this would be his final trip to Wembley was said in a jovial manner. It now seems that Guardiola has provided what could be considered a more definitive answer to the questions that surround his future in an interview with the BBC. What is apparent is that Pep Guardiola is sticking to the line that he has a contract for next season with Manchester City and it is beginning to feel like Manchester City will enter the 2026-27 season with Guardiola as manager.

In an interview with the BBC Sport that was released ahead of today’s FA Cup final, Pep Guardiola was directly asked if he will be the manager of Manchester City next season. Guardiola answered yes to the question. The full exchange can be seen below and you draw your own conclusions from Pep Guardiola’s answer and demeanour in the interview with BBC Sport.

Pep Guardiola has once again pointed out to BBC Sport that he is contracted to be Manchester City manager for next season. However, the standout feature of that interview is when Guardiola answers yes to the question of whether he will be City manager next season. That was my biggest takeaway from the clip that BBC Sport has released. I cannot see how Pep Guardiola’s answer can be taken any other way. Pep Guardiola’s frustration about being asked about his future is also evident in the BBC clip, so perhaps a more definitive answer as to whether or not Guardiola will be at Manchester City next season is coming.

Having said that, does Pep Guardiola need to provide one? He’s contracted for next season, as he has stated on multiple occasions. If he isn’t leaving Manchester City, Guardiola can just continue his work, and the status quo remains. The only statement that Guardiola will need to make is if he is leaving Manchester City at the end of this season. Based on his interview today, it does seem that Pep Guardiola will remain as City manager for next season.
